moves.name: Stand LP
moves.notes: Good neutral buffer into 5LP~LP Target Combo (requires at least a Counter-hit to combo); causes air reset if juggled into

moves.name: Stand MP
moves.notes: 2f extra recovery on whiff; great hitbox and a strong buffer into 5MP~MP Target Combo

moves.name: Stand HP
moves.notes: Can hold button 21f for improved properties; strong whiff punish tool

moves.name: Marisa Style (HP)
moves.notes: 1f extra recovery on whiff; releasing the button on frames 7-17 results in uncharged version with 5f startup; great range and frame advantage for follow-up combos and pressure; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state

moves.name: Stand LK
moves.notes: At farther ranges, can only combo into SA2; special cancel is delayed until after 2nd recovery frame; DR cancel is delayed until after 3rd recovery frame

moves.name: Stand MK
moves.notes: Good poke and Drive Rush starter

moves.name: Stand HK
moves.notes: Can hold button 17f for improved properties; solid poke with good hitbox priority; Punish Counter puts opponent into free juggle state; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; extends a hurtbox 1f before active that is vulnerable to projectiles

moves.name: Crouch LP
moves.notes: Chains into 2LP/2LK; Marisa's only 4f normal; special cancel is delayed until after active frames; DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd recovery frame

moves.name: Crouch MP
moves.notes: Long range for a cancelable 2MP (but slower than average startup); topmost hurtboxes on frames 8-15 are projectile invincible (avoids Sagat's MP/HP Tiger Shot)

moves.name: Crouch HP
moves.notes: Can hold button 17f for improved properties; slow but solid anti-air; cannot hit cross-up; only first 2 active frames hit grounded opponents; active frames 3-5 only hit airborne opponents; puts opponent into limited juggle state on hit (higher launch vs. airborne, and much higher on Punish Counter)

moves.name: Crouch HP (Hold)
moves.notes: Launches opponent into limited juggle state on hit, and much higher launch on Punish Counter; useful for continuing juggles; cannot hit cross-up and too slow to realistically anti-air; only first 2 active frames hit grounded opponents; active frames 3-6 only hit airborne opponents; releasing the button on frames 5-16 results in uncharged version with 4f startup; only Super cancelable on the first 2 active frames; can always combo into a fully charged SA1 for incredible 1-bar damage; hitstop is 2 frames longer on hit than on block for easier cancel hitconfirms

moves.name: Crouch LK
moves.notes: Chains into 2LP/2LK

moves.name: Crouch MK
moves.notes: Solid low poke with good range and hitbox priority; can lead to 5MP~MP combos out of Drive Rush at closer ranges; topmost hurtboxes on frames 9-16 are projectile invincible (avoids Sagat's MP/HP Tiger Shot but becomes vulnerable on frame 17~)

moves.name: Gaia Bash
moves.notes: Can hold button 20f for improved properties; has juggle potential; topmost hurtboxes on frames 1-7 are projectile invincible (avoids Sagat's MP/HP Tiger Shot)

moves.name: Jump LP
moves.notes: Quick air-to-air

moves.name: Jump MP
moves.notes: Puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; lower body projectile invuln 7-21f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

moves.name: Jump HP
moves.notes: Can hold button 20f for improved properties; causes spike knockdown vs. airborne opponents; lower body projectile invuln 9-23f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

moves.name: Marisa Style (j.HP)
moves.notes: Releasing the button on frames 5-22 results in uncharged version with 5f startup; causes spike knockdown; lower body projectile invuln 28-37f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

moves.name: Jump LK
moves.notes: Cross-up; useful for safe jumps; can be used as a fuzzy instant overhead

moves.name: Jump MK
moves.notes: Farthest reaching horizontal jump-in with good hitbox priority; lower body projectile invuln 8-25f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

moves.name: Jump HK
moves.notes: Can hold button 20f for improved properties; lower body projectile invuln 10-21f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

moves.name: Novacule 1
moves.notes: Strong forward movement with useful links out after Counter-hit, Punish Counter, or Drive Rush; has 2 hitconfirmable Target Combo follow-ups

moves.name: Magna Bunker
moves.notes: 3f extra recovery on whiff; can hold button 17f for improved properties; causes air reset when juggled into without Drive Rush; short range but important in combos and pressure; special/DR cancel is delayed until after 2nd recovery frame

moves.name: Magna Bunker (Hold)
moves.notes: 5f extra recovery on whiff; 2nd active frame is omitted on hit/block (when combined with the extra whiff recovery, the total frame count is 6f longer on whiff compared to hit/block); releasing the button on frames 5-16 results in uncharged version with 3f startup; short range but excellent combo and pressure tool; meaty connect does not give extra frame advantage; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state

moves.name: Malleus Breaker 1
moves.notes: Can hold button 17f for improved propertied; has a Target Combo follow-up; causes a short OTG bounce vs. airborne opponents

moves.name: Malleus Breaker 1 (Hold)
moves.notes: Slow overhead that can combo on hit or give pressure on block; releasing the button on frames 7-13 results in uncharged version with 14f startup; causes a short OTG bounce vs. airborne opponents

moves.name: Falx Crusher 1
moves.notes: Can hold button 21f for improved properties; has a hitconfirmable Target Combo follow-up; forces stand on hit

moves.name: Falx Crusher 1 (Hold)
moves.notes: Releasing the button on frames 7-14 results in uncharged version with 7f startup (frames 15-20: 4f startup); has a hitconfirmable Target Combo follow-up; forces stand on hit; puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; more range than uncharged version with excellent follow-up combos or block pressure; special/DR cancel is delayed until after 3rd active frame

moves.name: Caelum Arc
moves.notes: Can hit Cross-up; can hold button 20f for improved properties; causes spike knockdown vs. airborne opponents; lower body projectile invuln 9f until land except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

moves.name: Caelum Arc (Hold)
moves.notes: Can hit Cross-up; releasing the button on frames 5-24 results in uncharged version with 4f startup; causes spike knockdown; lower body projectile invuln 28f until land except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

moves.name: Light Two Hitter
moves.notes: Requires Counter-hit, Punish Counter, or Drive Rush 5LP to combo; 3f gap on block between hits that allows for a frame trap

moves.name: Medium Two Hitter
moves.notes: Great pressure and bully tool from 5MP; true blockstring between hits; special/DR cancel is delayed until after 1st recovery frame

moves.name: Heavy Two Hitter
moves.notes: () refers to scaled damage after 5HP combo starter; 6f gap between hits on block (1f gap if starting from 5); grants a safe jump near the corner on a grounded hit or low-height juggle

moves.name: Novacule Swipe
moves.notes: Low follow-up; true blockstring from 6MP, so an opponent mashing a crouching button after blocked 6MP will not be frame trapped; useful confirm if 6MP hits crouching opponent

moves.name: Novacule Thrust
moves.notes: Whiffs on crouching opponents; if the opponent is standing, this ender is preferable for its corner carry and Super cancelability; Super cancel is delayed until after 3rd active frame

moves.name: Malleus Breaker 2
moves.notes: Gives a risky 2nd overhead after 3MP; true blockstring, so opponent can safely mash a standing normal after blocking 3MP without being frame trapped or hit by the overhead; can be juggled into (KD +31)

moves.name: Falx Crusher 2
moves.notes: Hitconfirmable ender after 6HK; delaying the input can create a frame trap up to 3f on block (no gap if starting from 6); puts opponent into limited juggle state; extremely powerful when juggled into (does not increase juggle count at all from DR~6HK)

moves.name: Volare Combo
moves.notes: Spike KD vs. airborne opponents

moves.name: Mounted Grace
moves.notes: Can use Drive Rush for a corner throw loop with 2f timing on standard characters; a successful Throw Escape builds 1 Drive bar and 1000 Super gauge (10% bar); Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

moves.name: Ponte Milvio
moves.notes: Side switch; after throwing opponent into corner, can Drive Rush for strong strike/throw mixup; DR~5 trades with reversal 4f normals for a high-damage trade combo; meaty options are much weaker after normal dash (no throw, limited conversions on hit); a successful Throw Escape builds 1 Drive bar and 1000 Super gauge (10% bar); Throw Escape is awarded to a random player if input simultaneously

moves.name: Secutor
moves.notes: See Drive Impact. Airborne connect gives spinning juggle state on Punish Counter or corner Wall Splat with variable height.

moves.name: Love Tap (Block)
moves.notes: Performed by holding 6HPHK on wakeup; 5f extra recovery on hit; does not have any screen freeze; 4f less hitstop on block compared to most Drive Reversals (changes the punish timing); see Drive Reversal.

moves.name: Drive Parry
moves.notes: See Drive Parry. Perfect Parrying has only 1F of recovery and disables the opponent from canceling their attack. Perfect Parrying projectiles puts you into fixed 11 frames of recovery. After a Perfect Parry, all Super Gauge gain is reduced by 20% and Drive Gauge gain/damage is reduced by 50% for the entire punish combo.

moves.name: Parry Drive Rush
moves.notes: See Drive Rush; 1-bar version performed out of a Parry; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation (if performed immediately after a successful Parry, only 1+8 startup); the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties; Distance: 0.544 (min/throw), 1.903 (min/block), 3.081 (max/final DR frame)

moves.name: Drive Rush Cancel
moves.notes: See Drive Rush; 3-bar version performed on hit/block from a cancelable normal; can also be performed on whiff from cancelable light normals; startup refers to minimum time before an attack can cancel the Drive Rush animation; the first 15 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack; the final 22 recovery frames are cancelable into any attack or movement option or blocking (except Parry); only Normal and Command Normal attacks are enhanced with +4 frame advantage and improved juggle properties

moves.name: Gladius
moves.notes: Armor loses to low attacks; cannot hit cross-up; can hold the button 26f to charge a more powerful version (armor is lost immediately upon button release); after releasing partial charge, startup is 5f (frames 12-19) or 4f (frames 20-25); holding the button for 11-12f can extend the armor by 1-2f without increasing startup

moves.name: Gladius (Hold)
moves.notes: Armor loses to low attacks; transitions to a cinematic follow-up on hit; vs. airborne opponents, 2nd follow-up hit whiffs (no Hard Knockdown and reduced Super Meter gain); cannot hit cross-up

moves.name: Gladius
moves.notes: Armor loses to low attacks; cannot hit cross-up; can hold the button 29f to charge a more powerful version (armor is lost immediately upon button release); after releasing partial charge, startup is 7f (frames 12-19) or 6f (frames 20-28)

moves.name: Gladius
moves.notes: Armor loses to low attacks; cannot hit cross-up; can hold the button 35f to charge a more powerful version (armor is lost immediately upon button release); after releasing partial charge, startup is 10f (frames 12-19), 9f (frames 20-27), or 8f (frames 28-34); holding the button for 33-34f results in an uncharged 236HP with even slower startup than the fully charged version

moves.name: Gladius
moves.notes: Armor loses to low attacks; cannot hit cross-up; can hold the button 29f to charge a more powerful version (2nd hit of armor is lost immediately upon releasing both Punch buttons); after releasing partial charge, startup is 8f (frames 11-12), 7f (frames 13-19), or 6f (frames 20-28)

moves.name: Dimachaerus
moves.notes: Cannot hit cross-up; cancelable into 6P follow-up on hit or armor absorb; puts airborne opponents into free juggle state

moves.name: Dimachaerus
moves.notes: Cannot hit cross-up; cancelable into 6P follow-up on hit or armor absorb; puts airborne opponents into free juggle state

moves.name: Dimachaerus
moves.notes: Cannot hit cross-up; cancelable into 6P follow-up on hit or armor absorb; puts airborne opponents into free juggle state

moves.name: Dimachaerus
moves.notes: Cannot hit cross-up; cancelable into 6P follow-up on hit or armor absorb; puts airborne opponents into free juggle state

moves.name: LP Dimachaerus Follow-up
moves.notes: Only comes out after 214LP hits or is armor absorbed

moves.name: MP Dimachaerus Follow-up
moves.notes: Only comes out after 214MP hits or is armor absorbed; gives a slight ground bounce on hit (can follow up with juggle vs. airborne opponents)

moves.name: HP Dimachaerus Follow-up
moves.notes: Only comes out after 214HP hits or is armor absorbed

moves.name: OD Dimachaerus Follow-up
moves.notes: Only comes out after 214PP hits or is armor absorbed

moves.name: Phalanx
moves.notes: Cannot hit cross-up; hits 1f later vs. crouching opponents

moves.name: Phalanx
moves.notes: Cannot hit cross-up; hits 1f later vs. crouching opponents

moves.name: Phalanx
moves.notes: Cannot hit cross-up; hits 1f later vs. crouching opponents

moves.name: Phalanx
moves.notes: Sends opponent tumbling backward into OTG state; if opponent hits the corner, a low-height Wall Splat occurs (opponent is grounded and throwable, but strikes put them into juggle state); cannot hit cross-up; hits 1f later vs. crouching opponents

moves.name: Quadriga
moves.notes: Cannot hit cross-up; ~50% screen length

moves.name: Quadriga
moves.notes: Cannot hit cross-up; ~60% screen length

moves.name: Quadriga
moves.notes: Cannot hit cross-up; ~70% screen length

moves.name: Quadriga
moves.notes: Cannot hit cross-up; ~65% screen length; Wall Splat on hit; KD Advantage increases when farther from the wall; significant pushback on block makes it hard to punish when spaced out

moves.name: Scutum
moves.notes: Automatically triggers counterattack after 214K absorbs attack; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; can hold button to remain in Scutum stance after counterattack; hit advantage depends on attack that is absorbed; Marisa's short 6f hitstop causes attack to land as a +1 Counter-hit against most Medium/Heavy normals, while landing as a +3 Punish Counter against Lights

moves.name: Scutum
moves.notes: Automatically triggers counterattack after 214KK absorbs attack; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; can hold button to remain in Scutum stance after counterattack; hit advantage depends on attack that is absorbed; Marisa's short 6f hitstop causes attack to land as a +1 Counter-hit against most Medium/Heavy normals, while landing as a +3 Punish Counter against Lights

moves.name: Tonitrus 1
moves.notes: 1st active frame only hits airborne opponents (functionally 8+16f startup vs. grounded); causes spike knockdown vs. airborne opponents; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; hitconfirm window is 1f shorter vs. grounded opponents; cannot be canceled into Tonitrus 2 on whiff; range includes 0.218 forward movement from 214K startup

moves.name: Tonitrus 2
moves.notes: True blockstring from overhead starter; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; cannot be performed when Tonitrus 1 whiffs

moves.name: Procella
moves.notes: Risky low ender; may catch opponent by surprise since the overhead ender is safe on block; Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; range includes 0.218 forward movement from 214K startup

moves.name: Enfold
moves.notes: Marisa is in a Punish Counter state for entire duration; Range: 1.109 (final active frame: 1.164); range includes 0.218 forward movement from 214K startup

moves.name: Javelin of Marisa
moves.notes: Can hold button 27f for more damage and a cinematic counter that activates against strikes and throws

moves.name: Javelin of Marisa
moves.notes: Held version; only the first 3 active frames trigger cinematic hit, which deals extra damage and Drive damage; max-range non-cinematic version does not give a Hard Knockdown; if frames 1-27 absorb a strike or throw, results in Counterattack version instead; has slightly less range than the uncharged version

moves.name: Javelin of Marisa
moves.notes: Automatically triggers counterattack after Held SA1 armor absorbs a strike or throw on frames 1-27; can be baited with safe jump timing

moves.name: Meteorite
moves.notes: Useful anti-air, reversal, and juggle ender; can be steered by holding forward/back; this affects the range, allowing you to hold back for close anti-airs or hold forward in ranged juggles or fireball punishes; upward hit triggers a cinematic; on block/whiff, ends with a downward slam that does 500 less damage than the cinematic version (still does full Drive Damage on hit); Marisa's collision box extends far downward, preventing opponent from walking or dashing underneath (opponent can still jump to avoid 2nd hit)

moves.name: Goddess of the Hunt
moves.notes: Takes opponent to corner; available at 25% HP or below; cinematic time regenerates ~2.1 Drive bars for Marisa; excellent whiff punish and projectile counter tool (especially with Modern controls)
